_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
BREEAM is a voluntary third-party certification standard administered by BRE Global (Building Research Establishment), a UK-based private research body. It is not statutory regulation in itself, but has been adopted into mandatory planning conditions, grant funding requirements, and tenancy covenants across the UK, EU, and increasingly international markets. In this regulatory supply chain, the workspace — as a freight forwarder occupying or procuring warehouse, logistics hub, fine art storage, automotive preparation, or event staging facilities — sits at two distinct positions depending on the transaction type.

**Position 1 — Tenant / Occupier:** Where the workspace leases an existing or newly constructed facility, BREEAM certification is the landlord's or developer's obligation. However, lease clauses in 'green leases' increasingly require the occupier to cooperate with BREEAM In-Use assessments, provide operational energy and water data, and maintain fit-out standards consistent with the certified design. The workspace must confirm whether any current or prospective lease contains such cooperation obligations before signing.

**Position 2 — Developer / Client:** Where the workspace commissions a bespoke facility (e.g., a new temperature-controlled freight hub, fine art vault, or humanitarian pre-positioning depot) and is the developer of record or primary funder, it steps into the role of 'client' under the BREEAM scheme — the party responsible for instructing a licensed assessor, commissioning the LCA, and lodging registration. In this position, all V7 substantive obligations bind the workspace directly as the commissioning entity.

**International scope:** BREEAM International New Construction V7 applies in all countries where no local national adaptation exists. This is relevant for workspaces commissioning or occupying facilities across trade lanes in the Americas, Asia, and wider Europe. UK New Construction V7 applies specifically to commercial projects in England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland. The framework used at registration is locked in at that point and governs the entire assessment lifecycle.

Role placement requiring legal confirmation: Whether the workspace constitutes a 'developer/client' or a 'tenant' for any given asset, and whether any green lease cooperation obligation is enforceable under local law, should be confirmed by legal counsel before facility design is frozen or a lease is executed.

*Source: BREEAM New Construction V7 FAQs, BRE Global, 2025–2026. https://breeam.com/breeam-newconstructionv7/breeam-version-7-faqs; BREEAM New Construction Standard, BRE Global, 2025. https://breeam.com/standards/new-construction; BREEAM Knowledge Base — Registrations, BRE Global, updated 25 March 2026. https://kb.breeam.com/wp-content/plugins/breeamkb-pdf/pdf/?c=920*
